* feat: add proximity bias to neighborhood and MSA search suggestions

Add optional p_lat / p_lng parameters to the search_neighborhoods and
search_msas Supabase RPC functions.  When coordinates are supplied,

fix: correctly repair orphaned migrations in CI using error output (#108)

* fix: auto-repair orphaned remote migrations before db push in CI

When a migration is applied via db-migration-apply.yml, Supabase records
its version in the remote schema_migrations table. On the next PR, if the
runner checks out a branch that does not contain that migration file locally,
 fails with:
  'Remote migration versions not found in local migrations directory.'

Fix: add a 'Repair orphaned remote migrations' step (in both workflows)
that runs before db push / db push --dry-run. It uses  to find remote-only versions (LOCAL column empty, REMOTE
column non-empty) and calls
for each, removing the stale remote history entry so the push can proceed.

Also splits the  call into its own named step for clarity.

Co-authored-by: Jakobi Haskell <jakobi_haskell@brown.edu>

* fix: use error output to drive migration repair instead of parsing migration list

The previous fix used 'supabase migration list --linked' output parsed with
awk -F'│' (box-drawing char U+2502), which silently produced no output in
the GitHub Actions locale, so no repairs were made and db push --dry-run
continued to fail.

New approach: run db push / db push --dry-run first; if it fails with
'Remote migration versions not found', parse the repair command that
Supabase already prints in the error (grep -oP for the version number),
run 'supabase migration repair --status reverted <version>', then retry.
This is locale-independent and uses the exact versions Supabase reports.

Co-authored-by: Jakobi Haskell <jakobi_haskell@brown.edu>

feat: lobby-to-game flow — game state machine, action processing, progression & completion (#44)

* feat: add game state machine engine and action processing route

- Add GameErrorKeys to validation for typed game action errors
- Extend GamePhaseSchema with granular game flow phases
- Add pendingBuyTilePosition, lastDiceRoll, winnerId, eliminatedPlayerIds to GameStateSchema
- Create gameStateMachine.ts in shared: applyAction pure function handling
  roll_dice, buy_tile, decline_tile, end_turn, path_choice,
  develop_tile, mortgage_tile, redeem_tile with full rule enforcement
- Add POST /api/games/:id/action route in worker
- Update lobby start to initialize tiles, proper phase (waiting_for_roll),
  and action points for first player

Co-authored-by: Evans A. Stepanov <estepanov@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add comprehensive game action integration and unit tests

- 19 integration tests covering full lobby→game→action flow:
  auth, turn validation, roll_dice, buy_tile, decline_tile, end_turn,
  rent payment, doubles, special tiles, round cycling, mortgage/redeem,
  state endpoint verification, game log tracking
- 16 unit tests for applyAction state machine engine:
  movement, start bonus, turn validation, triple doubles, buy/decline,
  end_turn round advancement, mortgage/redeem, rent payment

Co-authored-by: Evans A. Stepanov <estepanov@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments — 4 bugs fixed

1. Fix double Free Market payout on diagonal overflow: when rolling off
   the diagonal to FREE MARKET, the pool was collected inline AND again
   in resolveLanding. Now skipLandingResolve prevents the second payout.

2. Disallow end_turn before rolling: removed waiting_for_roll from
   handleEndTurn's allowed phases. Players must complete mandatory
   movement step before ending their turn.

3. Validate action payloads with GameActionSchema: the worker route now
   runs Zod schema validation (GameActionSchema.safeParse) before calling
   applyAction, rejecting out-of-range dice values and unknown action types.

4. Implement waiting_for_path_choice: landing exactly on START now
   transitions to waiting_for_path_choice phase, making the diagonal
   path_choice action reachable during normal gameplay.

Added tests for all four fixes (4 unit + 3 integration).

Co-authored-by: Evans A. Stepanov <estepanov@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address round-2 PR review — regulation penalty, path-choice routing, diagonal overflow

1. Regulation penalty persists through next turn: inRegulation is no
   longer cleared at the end of the turn the player was sent to
   regulation. Instead, the penalized player gets 0 AP on their next
   turn (skipping optional actions per rules), and the flag clears
   after they complete that penalty turn.

2. Path-choice routing when passing through START: the engine now
   auto-rolls a path-choice die (odd=perimeter, even=diagonal) when
   a roll carries a player through position 0. Remaining movement is
   routed onto the chosen path, making the diagonal accessible during
   normal gameplay.

3. Diagonal overflow continues on perimeter: when rolling past D5,
   the player arrives at FREE MARKET (collecting the pool), then any
   remaining steps continue clockwise on the perimeter track from
   position 20, with normal landing resolution.

Added 8 new unit tests covering all three fixes.

Co-authored-by: Evans A. Stepanov <estepanov@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: make path-choice die deterministic for tests

The path-choice auto-roll when passing through START used Math.random(),
causing the 'collects start bonus when passing position 0' test to fail
non-deterministically when the die rolled even (routing to diagonal).

- Add optional pathChoiceDie field to GameActionInput so the server can
  inject a pre-rolled value and tests can control it deterministically
- Worker route pre-rolls rollPathChoiceDie() server-side and passes it
  into the engine input for roll_dice actions
- Update tests to pass explicit pathChoiceDie values (odd=perimeter,
  even=diagonal) with separate test cases for each path

Co-authored-by: Evans A. Stepanov <estepanov@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve CI typecheck failure

- Fix TS2352 type assertion error in games.ts action route by using
  spread + conditional object instead of Record<string, unknown> cast
- Exclude packages/worker/src/db/ from tsconfig (drizzle-orm scaffolding
  added in af9d27f without the dependency installed)

fix: eliminate orphan pages — fix ghost add_related_link, remove >=3 threshold, use structured data (#43)

P0: Replace dead add_related_link dispatch with organize(action='link'),
    and check return value before marking linked=True. Previously the
    dispatch silently returned an error string (not an exception),
    so linked=True was always set — pages appeared linked in reports
    but were actually orphaned.

P1: Remove the >=3 pages threshold for hub creation. Any tag without
    an existing hub now triggers hub creation, ensuring zero orphan pages.

P2: Replace fragile string-parsing in _ensure_progressive_disclosure_from_results
    with structured tool call data collected during execute_plan(). The old
    method parsed English result strings ('written to', 'created ... page at')
    to extract paths — now the executed tool calls with their original arguments
    are passed directly to _ensure_progressive_disclosure().

Tests: Strengthen test_orphan_page_linked_to_hub to verify actual file content
(hub contains [[wikilink]]) instead of just checking report text. Add new test

* fix: remove internal infrastructure terminology from observability references

Replace proprietary internal terms with generic equivalents:
- IFx -> legacy telemetry
- Geneva -> collector
- MDM/MDSD -> legacy telemetry SDKs
- dk8s_arch -> cpu_arch
- machine_sku -> instance_type

These were internal infrastructure terms that shouldn't appear in
a public MIT-licensed repository.

* fix: implement 4/5 skill pass gate with regression check

The apply_kill_gate function was incorrectly requiring ALL skills to pass
the 0.5 threshold, when the plan specifies:
- PROCEED: at least 4 of 5 skills pass >= 0.5 with no regression
- CONDITIONAL: 3 of 5 skills pass >= 0.5
- STOP: fewer than 3 skills pass OR any skill regresses

Changes:
- Track skills_passing count instead of failing on any single failure
- Add regression detection (delta < 0) separate from threshold check
- Implement three-tier verdict: PROCEED, CONDITIONAL, STOP
- Update output to show verdict and distinguish REGRESS vs BELOW threshold

* fix: correct kill gate logic and remove cross-skill dependency

Bug 1: apply_kill_gate now allows CONDITIONAL verdict when skills regress.
Previously, any regression unconditionally returned STOP, preventing
CONDITIONAL from being reached even when 3+ skills improved. The
regression check now only downgrades PROCEED to CONDITIONAL, not blocks
entirely.

Bug 2: Created tailored copy of Boy Scout Rule for decision-critic skill.
The cross-skill path ../analyze/references/quality-boy-scout-rule.md
violated the per-skill ownership model. Each skill now has its own copy
in its references/ directory.

* fix(eval): support dynamic skill discovery for custom prompts

The eval script hardcoded 5 skill paths. Now resolves any skill name
dynamically from .claude/skills/. Fixes --prompts-file not finding
extended skills (chaos-experiment, observability, etc.).

Also adds 48 prompts for 8 additional skills in eval-extended-prompts.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: require at least 1 skill to pass in kill gate for small skill counts

When evaluating fewer than 5 skills, the condition 'skills_passing >= total_skills - 1'
produced incorrect results. With 1 skill, this became 'skills_passing >= 0', which
is always true, allowing a skill with zero improvement to pass the gate.

Changed to 'skills_passing >= max(1, total_skills - 1)' to ensure at least one skill
must meet the 0.5 improvement threshold for the kill gate to pass.

* feat(skills): add niche/org-specific references (highest ROI batch)

Focus on content NOT in model pre-training per eval findings:
- Agent architecture patterns + context budget management (analyze, planner)
- Hybrid memory architecture for agents (planner)
- .NET monorepo standards + performance patterns (style-enforcement, code-qualities)
- Claude Code productivity patterns (context-optimizer)
- Codebase knowledge graph (memory)
- Autonomous execution guardrails (security-scan)

Eval showed references add value inversely proportional to pre-training
coverage. This batch targets niche agent-specific and org-specific knowledge
where the delta is highest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing reference files to SKILL.md See Also tables

- Add codebase-knowledge-graph.md to memory skill references
- Add claude-code-productivity-patterns.md to context-optimizer references

Both files were added to references/ directories but were not listed
in their respective SKILL.md files, violating AC-4 and preventing
demand-loading by agents.

* fix: remove duplicate reference listings in code-qualities-assessment SKILL.md

Remove calibration-examples.md and refactoring-patterns.md from the new
References table section as they are already listed in the existing
Scoring References details section. Only dotnet-performance-patterns.md
is genuinely new and should be in the References section.

* fix: defer API key loading until after dry-run check

The _load_api_key() call was running unconditionally before checking
args.dry_run, and it calls sys.exit(1) if no key is found. This meant
--dry-run mode would crash if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was not set, even
though the API key is never used during a dry run.

Now the API key is only loaded when not in dry-run mode, allowing
users to validate their prompt configuration without setting up an
API key.

* fix: address PR review feedback on reference files

- Fix MySQL least-privilege example to use subnet-restricted host instead of wildcard '%'
- Correct decision log entry: references/ not resources/ per SkillForge convention
- Fix prompt count in M3 deliverables: 30 prompts (6 per skill x 5 skills), not 15

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: strip surrounding quotes from .env file values

The custom .env parser now strips matching surrounding single or double
quotes from values, consistent with standard .env parsers like dotenv.
This prevents API authentication failures when users write their API key
in the common ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..." format.

* fix: add HTTP error handling and robust JSON parsing to eval script

The eval script lacked error handling for API failures (429, 500, etc.),
which would crash mid-run and lose all prior results. Also fixed fragile
code block parsing that failed when the model included a language tag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: scale kill gate thresholds proportionally and rename shadowed variable

- Apply proportional thresholds (80% for PROCEED, 60% for CONDITIONAL) in
  apply_kill_gate() to support any number of skills, not just 5

  shadowing the request body variable

* fix: replace org-specific IC levels with industry-standard engineering tiers

Replace IC59-IC68+ and MSec SWE expectations with Entry/Junior/Senior/Staff/
Principal levels aligned with industry conventions. Each tier now describes
autonomy level and focus area in terms meaningful to any engineer.

Addresses PR review comment r3068393281.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use math.ceil for kill gate thresholds to enforce documented 80%/60% criteria

int() truncates toward zero, making thresholds systematically too lenient.
For example, with 12 skills: int(12 * 0.8) = 9 (75%) instead of 10 (80%).
math.ceil correctly rounds up to enforce 'at least X%' as documented.

* fix: research-backed engineering tiers with Mid-Level and corrected YOE bands

Revised with industry research (staffeng.com, Levels.fyi, Dreyfus model):
- Added Mid-Level tier (3-5 years) that was missing
- Corrected experience bands to match industry consensus

Both copies of engineering-complexity-tiers.md contained unresolved git stash
conflict markers (<<<<<<< Updated upstream, ||||||| Stash base, =======,
>>>>>>> Stashed changes). Resolved conflicts by selecting the industry-standard
experience level content and removing org-specific IC level terminology.

* fix: display dynamic kill gate thresholds and correct regression behavior description

The summary table now calculates thresholds dynamically based on total_skills
using math.ceil(total_skills * 0.8) and math.ceil(total_skills * 0.6), matching
the actual logic in apply_kill_gate(). Also corrects the misleading text about

- /spec: analyst subagent classifies problem tier before writing requirements
- /review: analyst subagent assesses diff tier to calibrate review depth

* fix: remove stale eval result files with incorrect kill gate verdicts

The eval result JSON files (eval-analyze.json, eval-threat-modeling.json,
eval-golden-principles.json) contained kill_gate verdicts that contradict
the current apply_kill_gate function logic.

For single-skill evaluations where overall_delta < 0.5:
- proceed_threshold = max(1, ceil(1 * 0.8)) = 1
- conditional_threshold = max(1, ceil(1 * 0.6)) = 1
- skills_passing = 0 (since delta < 0.5)
- Result should be: passed=false, verdict=STOP

But the committed files showed:
- eval-analyze.json: passed=true, verdict=CONDITIONAL (delta -0.56)
- eval-threat-modeling.json: passed=true, verdict=PROCEED (delta 0.0)
- eval-golden-principles.json: passed=true, verdict=PROCEED (delta 0.39)

These files were generated by a previous version of the kill gate logic
and never regenerated. Keeping eval-decision-critic.json as it correctly
shows passed=true for a skill with delta 0.61 >= 0.5 threshold.

commit.message:
Add @prosemark/latex for $...$ math in Markdown (#120)

* feat: add @prosemark/markdown-latex for dollar-delimited math

Introduce a Lezer markdown extension for $...$ and 9429...9429 formulas plus
CodeMirror decorations that render via MathJax (SVG by default, CHTML
when output is html). MathJax loads from a pinned jsDelivr URL with
skipStartupTypeset for programmatic tex2svg/tex2chtml calls.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* refactor: rename package to @prosemark/latex and extend demos

- Move packages/markdown-latex to packages/latex with new public API names
  (latexMathMarkdownSyntaxExtension, latexMarkdownEditorExtensions, etc.)
- Style $/17179 delimiters vs formula body via Lezer tags + HighlightStyle
- Call view.requestMeasure after display-math render; set estimatedHeight
- Wire LaTeX into demo, docs ProseMark demo, debug web app, VS Code webview
- Add sample formulas to init docs; register TypeDoc entry for the package

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(latex): load MathJax from npm, add render LRU cache and README

- Replace CDN script injection with dynamic import of mathjax/tex-svg.js
  or tex-chtml.js; add module declarations for TypeScript
- Add renderCacheSize option (default 128) storing cloned output per formula
- Document install, options, and caching tradeoffs in README
- Depend on mathjax@^4.1.1

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix: restore MathJax init and normal arrow-up past math

- Pre-load only skipStartupTypeset on window.MathJax; full tex/svg config
  must come from MathJax after load (plain object was merged into config and
  broke tex2svgPromise)
- Mark latex replace decorations with proseMarkSkipAdjacentArrowReveal;
  revealBlockOnArrow skips them so blank lines after math are not skipped
- Note MathJax pre-config pitfall in README; changeset bumps core + latex

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* style(latex): blue delimiters, neutral formula source color

Use --pm-link-color for $/25185; formula body inherits editor text (override via
--pm-latex-math-formula-color).

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(latex): set MathJax loader root for bundled components

Bundled tex-svg/tex-chtml default loader.paths.mathjax to '/', so runtime
fetches hit the app origin and tex2svgPromise never resolves (empty widgets).

Configure loader.paths.mathjax to jsDelivr for the pinned version; add optional
mathJaxPackageUrl for self-hosting. Verified with headless Chrome on demo preview.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(latex): remeasure editor when block math DOM resizes

MathJax fills widgets asynchronously without a doc transaction, so CM6 never
set mustMeasureContent and block height stayed at estimatedHeight.

Set viewState.mustMeasureContent and requestMeasure after render; observe
wrapper with ResizeObserver for late layout (fonts) and disconnect on destroy.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(latex): run EditorView.measure after async math DOM updates

requestMeasure() alone can leave block heights stale; the full measure loop
must run with mustMeasureContent so measureVisibleLineHeights re-reads widgets.

Use double rAF + offsetHeight flush before calling internal measure(true).
Document console monkey-patch for debugging remeasure flow.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(core): resync fold gutter when editor geometry changes

foldGutter’s marker plugin ignored height-only updates, so async block
widgets left gutter element heights stale vs viewportLineBlocks — misaligned
fold icons and wrong lineBlockAtHeight for clicks.

Use foldingChanged: (u) => u.geometryChanged to rebuild markers whenever
layout/height changes.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* fix(latex): align block math layout with CodeMirror widget rules

Remove internal measure/mustMeasureContent hacks and foldGutter foldingChanged
workaround. Use padding instead of vertical margins on block math widgets per
Decoration docs, and rely on requestMeasure after async render and on resize.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* feat(core): add proseMathMarkdownSyntaxExtension to default markdown

Move dollar-delimited math parsing to core as Math/MathMark/MathFormula nodes,
include in prosemarkMarkdownSyntaxExtensions, add @lezer/highlight dependency.

Latex package re-exports latex* aliases, folds Math nodes with hybrid block rules.
Demos: drop redundant latex syntax import. Docs: mention prose math + latex widgets.

Co-authored-by: J. Simon Richard <jsimonrichard@gmail.com>

* refactor(core): rename proseMath* to math*; drop latex from vscode core

- mathDelimiterTag, mathFormulaTag, mathMarkdownSyntaxExtension (file mathMarkdown.ts)
- vscode-prosemark: remove @prosemark/latex dependency and webview latex extensions
- Update docs link slug and changeset

fix(auth): production login — session user id + redirect callback (#186)

* fix(auth): expose user id in session and correct NextAuth redirects

- Add jwt/session callbacks so session.user.id matches Prisma user id for getActiveUser
- Fix redirect callback to allow path-only callbackUrl (default NextAuth behavior)
- Set anon_id cookie secure only in production for local HTTP compatibility
- Extend next-auth Session type for optional user id

* fix(auth): align NEXTAUTH_URL with Vercel preview and deployment URL

- Resolve canonical origin from VERCEL_URL on preview/development
- Fallback to VERCEL_URL when VERCEL=1 and NEXTAUTH_URL is unset
- Load ensureNextAuthUrl before authOptions so getServerSession sees same URL
- Add unit tests for resolveNextAuthUrl

* fix(auth): run ensureNextAuthUrl on Node server startup via instrumentation

getServerSession() can load authOptions without executing the auth route module,
so NEXTAUTH_URL was not always normalized before JWT decode. Import
ensureNextAuthUrl in instrumentation register() so every Node worker sets it.

* fix(db): begrens pg-pool op Vercel en vang DB-timeouts in credentials-login af

Gebruik een expliciete pool-config (max 5 op Vercel, configureerbaar via PG_POOL_MAX)
voor PrismaPg om connection storms te verminderen. Credentials authorize vangt
Prisma-fouten (zoals ETIMEDOUT), logt naar Sentry en geeft null i.p.v. een
ruwe error-pagina.

* fix: CodeRabbit — Sentry bij redirect/URL-parse, anon_id secure, http(s) origin

- nextauth redirect: log invalid URL naar Sentry (geen stille catch)
- link/complete: zelfde secure-cookie gedrag als middleware (alleen productie)
